results.title: Crunchyroll's Late-Night Dark Action Masterpiece Is Perfect From Start to Finish, No Notes
results.body:
One of the greatest dark action TV shows is just waiting to be binge-watched (again) on Crunchyroll. The anime streaming service is home to some of the best animated action series around. There are contemporary favorites, like Demon Slayer and Chainsaw Man, alongside cult classics, like the '90s Berserk adaptation, Cowboy Bebop, and Dragon Ball Z. But one of the best among them, which doesn't have a single bad episode among its three seasons (and counting), is the perfect long weekend binge-watch.

Debuting in 2020, it shouldn't come as a surprise that Jujutsu Kaisen is one of the best action series on Crunchyroll. Based on the Shōnen Jump manga by Gege Akutami, the dark fantasy action series is set in a world where negative energy coagulates into curses, dark entities that feed on people's negativity. After ingesting a finger of Sukuna, one of the most powerful curse users to have ever existed, Itadori and the members of Jujutsu High race to find the other 19 fingers to destroy Sukuna once and for all. Of course, it's not that simple, as a cult of evil curse users are plotting to take over the world and create a utopia for curses, seeing them as superior beings.

Three seasons of Jujutsu Kaisen have been released to date, and the series keeps getting better. Given how popular the manga was, the anime was always going to be successful. But when Studio MAPPA threw everything and the kitchen sink at the series, the anime's popularity boomed, far exceeding the manga. The series has evolved drastically from its humble Shōnen beginnings. But that's what makes it so good.

The Evolution of 'Jujutsu Kaisen' x Your browser does not support the video tag.

Before you rush to the comments to tell us that Jujutsu Kaisen isn't a masterpiece (which is a subjective label in itself), hear us out. Season 1 was a fantastic Shōnen series that occasionally pushed its boundaries. The debut season wasn't anything revolutionary, but its execution of the standard Shōnen tropes was masterful. Then came Jujutsu Kaisen 0, the prequel movie. That was the turning point for the franchise. From Jujutsu Kaisen 0, the series embraced more of a cinematic aesthetic. Season 2 slowly implemented this, experimenting with framing, aspect ratios, blur, and other realistic animation techniques. But Season 3 has taken that aesthetic to the next level.

The common consensus among Jujutsu Kaisen fans is that Season 3 is "pure cinema" (we know you're picturing the Martin Scorsese meme). The third season, comprising only 12 episodes, is one of the best seasons of anime in recent years. Adapting the infamous Culling Game Arc, where nobody is safe, every episode has the production quality and sensibilities of a feature-length film, and it's completely captivating.

'Jujutsu Kaisen' Season 3, Part 2, When?

Unfortunately, Jujutsu Kaisen's third season won't be receiving a second part (or "cour," in anime speak). 12 episodes is the new trend for anime seasons, with shows like Dandadan, Chainsaw Man, and Season 2 of Frieren: Beyond Journey's End following suit. Instead of a second cour, Jujutsu Kaisen's story will pick up directly with a fourth season. The upcoming season will continue the overall Culling Game Arc, as Jujutsu Kaisen gears up for the beginning of the end. Jujutsu Kaisen Season 4 doesn't yet have a release date.

results.title: CCTV expansion in Selayang to curb illegal dumping
results.body:
Selayang Municipal Council (MPS) is looking to expand closed-circuit TV (CCTV) monitoring to curb persistent illegal dumping and waste problems.

Council president Shahman Jalaludin said the move was among several proposals raised during a three-day workshop in April involving MPS departments, councillors and KDEB Waste Management (KDEBWM).

"The workshop examined on-ground issues and generated a range of solutions covering short-, medium- and long-term measures.

"Among the key proposals was increasing the number of CCTV cameras and integrating them into the MPS Command Centre to improve monitoring and enforcement.

"There are 700,000 residents in Selayang.

"Manpower is limited and we cannot cover all areas in the municipality," he said after the monthly full board meeting at Menara MPS in Bandar Baru Selayang, Selangor.

Shahman said MPS was reviewing all proposals before deciding which ones to implement this year.

"A follow-up meeting will be held next week to finalise the proposals and determine budget requirements for implementation," he said.

He assured that the local council would focus on improving public awareness, strengthening enforcement and enhancing internal work processes.

"Waste-related complaints surged in the first quarter of the year, driven partly by festive periods such as Thaipusam, Chinese New Year and Hari Raya," said Shahman.

"MPS is hoping the measures implemented will curb illegal dumping and improve overall cleanliness across Selayang."

MPS' Solid Waste Management and Public Health Department issued 58 compounds based on recordings in its CCTV monitoring system up to March.

Last year, 264 compounds were issued, compared with 501 in 2024.

According to MPS, the cameras are in 12 locations: Jalan SBC 9; Lorong SBC 1; Rawang Millennium; Jalan Meranti in Rawang; Jalan Gombak; Jalan BRP 8/1 in Bukit Rahman Putra; Jalan Melati 20 in Batu Caves; Jalan Batu Caves in Gombak; Kota Emerald; Jalan TIB 1/5; Jalan SJ 1 in Taman Selayang Jaya and Pangsapuri Harmonis Gombak.

Meanwhile MPS Zone 3 councillor Mazlan Musa urged KDEBWM contractors to work closely with councillors to improve coordination while streamlining operations. -- By MEGAT SYAHAR

results.title: Dismantling Deltopia | The Daily Nexus
results.body:
Isla Vista streets left empty on what would've been Deltopia, with few arrests and citations issued.

The typical jam-packed streets, loud music and hundreds of citations seen at Deltopia celebrations in years past were noticeably absent from Del Playa Drive on April 4. Instead, 300 law enforcement officers from across California helped enforce the Isla Vista Foot Patrol's 72-hour noise ordinance, resulting in an over 90% decrease in medical calls, citations and arrests.

In January, the Santa Barbara County (SBC) Board of Supervisors unanimously adopted Isla Vista Foot Patrol's (IVFP) proposed ordinance banning amplified music during the first weekend of spring quarter. IVFP employed the same number of officers as last year to enforce the ordinance during the weekend in case crowds remained the same.

Deltopia, the yearly unsanctioned street festival, has drawn thousands of attendees in years past. Over the last four years, Deltopia's citations and arrests increased, with 2025 seeing a record 130 medical calls, 485 citations and 84 arrests among an estimated 25,000 attendees.

In contrast, celebrations this year resulted in seven medical calls, 42 citations and six arrests, according to a county press release. Roughly 12,000 people attended Isla Vista Community Services District's (IVCSD) alternative event, Soltopia, from 12-6 p.m.

I.V. beaches were closed for the whole weekend and vehicles were unable to enter Sabado Tarde Road and Del Playa Drive until after 8 p.m. on Saturday. Additionally, the Restorative Justice Program, which allows people to waive certain citations after completion of classes and community service, was suspended. Individuals who received citations can contact the Associated Students (A.S.) Legal Resource Center for free legal consultation.

At a special meeting of the SBC Board of Supervisors on April 7, Second District Supervisor Laura Capps commended IVFP and the Sheriff's Department for their handling of the weekend.

"There were so many people that were there for the health and safety of young people that call Isla Vista home, and it was remarkable to see," Capps said. "I want to note that [law enforcement] teams were out there, many of whom on a holiday weekend for them -- Easter weekend -- and that's a sacrifice."

A.S. External Vice President for Local Affairs (EVPLA) and third-year political science major E.J. Raad strongly advocated against the ordinance before its passage and said his opinion hasn't changed.

"There was such a disproportionate amount of police officers to students and there's obviously a lot more police officers than EMT or medical services," Raad said. "One of my biggest concerns was that this was just going to increase police power here in a way that didn't exist before, where we now have a mechanism where they can shut down a weekend and then also move the ordinance."

University of California Isla Vista, an organization under the EVPLA's office, set up tents along Del Playa Drive and at the Pardall Center on Saturday. While these tents have been a heavily utilized resource during past Deltopia weekends by providing food, water and escort services, Raad said that they had "pretty minimal interactions" on Del Playa, with about 250 people visiting the tent at Walter Capps Park.

Raad said that the suspension of restorative justice and the "disproportionate" police presence show that the ordinance is "just a way to make it easier for [law enforcement] to deploy those resources in a punitive way." He also highlighted that having the same number of law enforcement as last year goes against the goal of reducing Deltopia costs, unless IVFP commits to their promise of scaling down in the future.

Fourth-year history major Molly Vestal currently lives on Del Playa Drive and found the massive police presence "a bit jarring." She said she felt forced to find a coworker to cover her Saturday shift because of the barricades stopping her from reentering Del Playa until after 8 p.m.

"I'm used to seeing cops on [Del Playa]. I think that's just a normal part of life here, but it was an influx of just giant groups of 10 walking together at a time, looking for something to do, and I felt scared," Vestal said. "I was just walking down the street with a bag in my hand and I was like, 'Wait, are they gonna think that this is alcohol?' I'm super clumsy, and if I trip in front of them are they gonna think that I'm drunk and arrest me?"

Vestal and her housemate, Santa Barbara City College student Miranda Bohen, felt that communication around parking was confusing. Bohen said that the information she got from people around her made it unclear whether Del Playa and Sabado Tarde residents would be allowed to park on the street and leave and come back. Similar to Vestal, she felt that the large police presence was "nerve-racking."

"They canceled Deltopia and did all the ordinances for the wrong reasons. They just want to have control over us basically instead of really caring about anything else," Bohen said. "It sounds silly, but I feel like they really just don't want us to have fun instead of caring about us personally."

Going forward, Raad thinks there should be continued pushback against the ordinance and over-policing in I.V.

"I think that's something that's worth probably a future EVPLA advocating for," Raad said. "I'm nearing the end of my term, but that is a long-term project that I think is worth pursuing for sure. We just have to see how things proceed from here, but obviously this first year, it's exactly what we said was going to happen. It was just like they didn't save any money or any resources."

The Daily Nexus was unable to contact IVFP for comment.

results.title: Marshall looks for Blevins as spark to start important series at James Madison - WV MetroNews
results.body:
In less than three weeks, 10 of 14 Sun Belt Conference teams will partake in the league tournament in Montgomery, Ala.

Whether or not one is Marshall largely depends on how the Herd fares from here on out.

After splitting two games earlier this week, Marshall hits the road for an important three-game conference series at James Madison starting Friday at 6 p.m.

Game 2 is set for 4 p.m. Saturday with the series finale at 1 p.m. Sunday.

Marshall (20-24, 10-11) followed Tuesday's 9-6 loss at Morehead State with a 5-2 victory against Ohio.

JMU (18-25, 8-13) lost earlier this week to Virginia Tech, 14-1.

Marshall is among a sixth-place logjam in the Sun Belt standings with four other teams -- Arkansas State, Texas State, UL Monroe and Georgia State.

JMU (18-25, 8-13) is two games back of that group and next-to-last in the league standings.

Marshall seeks another strong performance Friday from starting pitcher Bryce Blevins, who has an 8-2 record and pitched a complete game a week ago in a victory against Troy at Jack Cook Field.

Blevins leads the country with 81 innings pitched after back-to-back complete games. He has recorded 71 strikeouts, fourth most in the Sun Belt. His eight wins lead the conference as he sits at a career of 20 with his next one to set the program career record.

The Herd offense has had a few standouts but has struggled to plate runs in recent games. Evan Bottone is fifth in the SBC with a .371 batting average, while Cooper Hinson holds the team lead with 17 doubles and is tied for third in the conference, 26th in the country. Tyler Kamerer has 48 runs and leads the Herd and is second in the Sun Belt. AJ Havrilla ranks 12th nationally with 43 walks during the 2026 season and leads the Sun Belt.

Marshall Head Coach Greg Beals says that an early offensive explosion in Wednesday's Ohio win which featured a grand-slam home run in the first inning by Jackson Halter was a good sign of improvement and got rid of a lot of frustration.

"It's good to be on the winning side of a ballgame, but more importantly I like how we won," Beals said. "We pitched the ball extremely well. You get the big hit from Halter in the first inning to put four runs on the board. Then our pitching staff for the most part took care of business."

It's been a tough season for James Madison, but the Dukes have the second most sacrifice bunts in the nation with 55 and the sixth most stolen bases at 127, led by Reece Moody's 30 stolen bases, a mark that leaves him No. 10 nationally.

results.title: Jujutsu Kaisen: Gege Akutami Officially Reveals The Strongest Mahoraga User in Modulo Volume 3
results.body:
Rei Penber is the Deputy Lead Editor for GameRant's Anime and Manga team, originally from Kashmir and currently based in Beirut. He brings seven years of professional experience as a writer and editor, five of which have been spent shaping the editorial standard at GameRant. Prior to his current role, he served as a Staff Writer at CBR (Comic Book Resources). Rei specializes in all things anime and manga, delivering industry-leading coverage for series like One Piece, Naruto, Jujutsu Kaisen, Solo Leveling, among many others. Rei excels at deep analysis of lore and intricate breakdowns of the narrative, along with high-level powerscaling. When he isn't working to improve GameRant's coverage of the latest developments in the anime and the manga world, he is re-reading his favorite story of all time, One Piece.

Jujutsu Kaisen: Modulo Volume 3 is set to be released on May 1, 2026, and with it, Gege Akutami is set to reveal a lot of exciting new information pertaining to the world, plot, and characters involved in Jujutsu Kaisen Modulo. Some supplementary information from JJK Modulo Volume 3 has already started coming up, and the author of the series, Gege Akutami, has revealed a lot of exciting details about the characters heavily involved in the volume, specifically the duo of Yuka and Tsurugi Okkotsu.

In JJK Modulo Volume 3, Gege Akutami officially expanded upon the lore surrounding the character of Yuka Okkotsu, and her overpowered cursed technique -- The Ten Shadows. Through Ten Shadows, a technique passed through the Zenin clan, the ultimate Shikigami, called Mahoraga, was summoned by Yuka in the battle against Dabura. So far, Mahoraga has had three confirmed users, each with differing levels of power. Gege Akutami has now finally answered who possesses the strongest Mahoraga, and the answer to that question is Yuka Okkotsu.

Every Main Jujutsu Kaisen Character's Age, Height, And Birthday

Jujutsu Kaisen is among the biggest ongoing anime. Here are a few basic details fans should know about the main characters as they join the adventure.

Posts 5

By Suzail Ahmad

Jujutsu Kaisen Modulo Volume 3 Extra Content Confirms Yuka To Be In Possesion Of The Strongest Mahoraga

Mahoraga is the ultimate trump card of the Ten Shadows Technique, which is passed down the Zenin bloodline. Megumi Fushiguro was the first character revealed to be able to use it, and he unleashed this power for the very first time during the Shibuya Incident arc against Haurta. Megumi, however, could not control it.

Later on, when Sukuna switched vessels from Yuji to Megumi, he also acquired the ability to make use of the Ten Shadow and, in turn, Mahoraga. It's only through the powers granted by Mahoraga that Sukuna was able to adapt to Limitless and defeat Satoru Gojo. This goes to show the overpowered strengths of this ability.

Megumi and Sukuna were both, undoubtedly, great users of Mahoraga, but in the most recent Jujutsu Kaisen Modulo Volume 3 content revealed by Gege Akutami and Yuji Iwasaki, it has now been officially confirmed that out of the three Mahoraga seen in the story so far, the strongest of them all belongs to neither Megumi nor Sukuna. Instead, it is Yuka Okkotsu who has the strongest Mahoraga.

"There is no difference in power between the respective Mahoraga belonging to Megumi, Sukuna, and Yuka. However, due to the prolonged nature of the battle, Yuka's Mahoraga ultimately emerged as the strongest of the three. " -- Gege Akutami in JJK Modulo Volume 3

Yuka Okkotsu is one of the main characters of Modulo, and she was revealed to have been the new inheritor of the Ten Shadows after Megumi. Against Dabura, she summoned the Divine General, and that's where it all shifted. Gege revealed that, at base, all three Mahoragas are technically equal; however, due to the length and difficulty of Mahoraga's battle against Dabura, it came to adapt to such a degree that it became superior to the Mahoragas that Sukuna and Megumi wielded previously.

Why Sukuna Remains The Strongest Mahoraga Master

When it comes to the Mahoraga themselves, Yuka's is now confirmed as the very strongest, topping those that Sukuna and Megumi made use of. However, it must be said that technically the only character ever known to tame the Divine General was none other than Sukuna. In that sense, Sukuna still technically has the edge against Yuka, as he can actually use and fight with the Divine General without the risk of it turning against him and attacking him. Yuka and Megumi, meanwhile, can only make use of Mahoraga by hiding in the shadows or by avoiding its attacks.

That isn't due to Yuka or Megumi's own power levels, but it simply comes down to the nature of the Ten-Shadow technique itself, which requires its user to make use of weaker Shikigamis to defeat an entity such as Mahoraga, which can adapt to anything thrown its way.

Overall, this new revelation by Gege Akutami confirms that out of three users, Megumi is undoubtedly at the bottom of the barrel. The fact that Gege didn't even bother mentioning him in the extra content is further proof of that. Theoretically, Megumi could've gained access to a tamed Mahoraga through Sukuna doing it in his body, but unless the author explicitly confirms it, nothing can be said for sure.

results.title: EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution: What are the best options for this 100,000 coins EVO?
results.body:
EA Sports has kicked off the Bundesliga Team of the Season promo with the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution, allowing gamers to upgrade their right-wingers on the virtual pitch. While the EVO requires either 100,000 coins or 500 FC Points to be unlocked, there are some top-tier options like Yamal, Gervinho and others that make it worth the investment.

The Bundesliga TOTS event has added a host of overpowered items to the game, along with an amazing Lennart Karl SBC as well. While Karl will prove to be an exceptional right midfielder or CAM on the virtual pitch, the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution will allow fans to create their very own elite-tier attackers as an alternative.

All requirements of the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution

A player must meet the following requirements to be eligible for the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution:

* Overall rating: Max 90

* Position: RM or RW

* PlayStyles: Max 10

* Playstyles+: Max two

* Must not be: World Tour Silver Superstars

Best players to use in the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution

These are some of the best right-wingers who meet these requirements and can be used in this EVO:

* Lamine Yamal: 90

All these players will reach three PlayStyles+ and receive massive stat boosts via this EVO.

All upgrades and challenges of the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution

Unlike most major EVOs released during the Premier League and Serie A TOTS promos, which had five levels each, the EA FC 26 Cut In, Say Less Evolution only has four levels. These are the boosts offered by each level:

Level 1 upgrades:

* Finesse Shot+ PlayStyle

* Sprint Speed +17 (Max 91)

* Acceleration +17 (Max 93)

* Vision +17 (Max 93)

* Crossing +17 (Max 90)

* FK Accuracy +17 (Max 89)

Level 2 upgrades:

* Pinged Pass PlayStyle

* Rapid PlayStyle

* Short Passing +17 (Max 92)

* Balance +17 (Max 92)

* Long Passing +17 (Max 91)

Level 3 upgrades:

* Skill moves +4 star

* Reactions +17 (Max 91)

* Ball control +17 (Max 91)

* Composure +17 (Max 91)

* Dribbling +17 (Max 92)

These are the challenges fans will have to complete across multiple Ultimate Team game modes to completely upgrade the player of their choice:

Level 1 challenges:

* Play one match in Squad Battles on minimum Semi Pro difficulty (or Rush/Rivals/Champions/Live Events) using your active EVO player.

Level 2 challenges:

* Play one match in Squad Battles on minimum Semi Pro difficulty (or Rush/Rivals/Champions/Live Events) using your active EVO player.

* Play one match in Squad Battles on minimum Semi Pro difficulty (or Rush/Rivals/Champions/Live Events) using your active EVO player.

* Play two matches in Squad Battles on minimum Semi Pro difficulty (or Rush/Rivals/Champions/Live Events) using your active EVO player.

Overall, the EVO is worth completing.

results.title: Top Cloud Phone Systems 2026: Best VoIP Solutions Tested by Our Experts
results.body:
The appropriate platform selection requires an evaluation of key business objectives. The two platforms that enterprises need to evaluate include RingCentral and 8x8. Startups can rely on Google Voice. Organizations that develop AI solutions and productivity enhancements should consider Dialpad as their primary option. Businesses that want to scale, improve customer communication, and support remote teams should invest in a cloud phone system in 2026.

A cloud phone system uses the internet to manage business calls instead of physical hardware. It enables calling, messaging, and collaboration across devices and locations easily.

2. Why are businesses switching to VoIP in 2026?

Businesses adopt VoIP for cost savings, remote accessibility, scalability, and advanced features like AI analytics, integrations, and global communication without heavy infrastructure investments.

3. Which is the best VoIP provider in 2026?

RingCentral leads overall, while Zoom Phone suits budget users, Dialpad excels in AI features, and 8x8 works best for global communication needs.

4. Are cloud phone systems reliable?

Most leading VoIP platforms offer high uptime and reliability. Performance depends on internet quality, provider infrastructure, and proper configuration within the organization's network setup.

5. How do I choose the right cloud phone system?

Evaluate pricing, scalability, integrations, call quality, and support. Choose a system that aligns with business size, communication needs, and future growth plans.

results.title: All FC 26 1 of 3 SA/PL/BL TOTS Champions Player Pick SBC rewards
results.body:
The 1 of 3 SA/PL/BL TOTS Champions Player Pick SBC is a unique way to get a new Red card for your FC 26 Ultimate Team squad. Ordinarily, you'll need to participate and do well in the Champions mode to get these cards. However, EA Sports has offered you an additional way to obtain these items.

Since you'll need to submit three TOTS Champions cards, it's always important to review the rewards before you spend your resources.

The 1 of 3 SA/PL/BL TOTS Champions Player Pick SBC features Champions cards from three different leagues.

Once you complete the SBC, you will have three options to choose from. The card you choose will be added to your squad, while the remaining two will be discarded. This SBC is a good way to recycle cards you don't need and find something useful. However, what you get ultimately depends on your luck, so there's an element of risk involved in the process.
